picks.reason: Sonny Gray’s SIERA/xFIP profile is nearly a full run better than Trey Yesavage’s, and Boston’s leverage arms are fresher. Toronto’s offense carries the platoon edge, but Fenway’s low HR factor and soggy south wind suppress extra-base noise. Yesavage’s small-sample aggregate keeps the edge below the betting gate.

Milwaukee’s 136 wRC+ against lefties dwarfs Cleveland’s 48, giving the Brewers a massive matchup edge at the plate. Both starters carry small-sample flags and Cleveland’s bullpen is fully rested, trimming the margin. Milwaukee’s own high-leverage fatigue keeps this from clearing the gate.

Joe Ryan’s strike-zone dominance and SIERA gap over a degrading Jack Leiter is the largest pitching mismatch on the slate, amplified by Globe Life’s pitcher-friendly dimensions. Minnesota’s missing closer and a rested Texas bullpen offset some of that advantage, leaving the edge short of four points.

Bryan Woo’s elite SIERA/xFIP and recent form give Seattle the starter edge over a scuffling Shane Baz, and T-Mobile Park suppresses offense. Baltimore’s fresher bullpen and slightly stronger platoon numbers against righties claw back most of the gap.

Sean Manaea’s aggregate SIERA is more than half a run lower than Aaron Nola’s, and Nola’s recent walk-driven degradation adds another downgrade. The Mets also carry the platoon edge against Nola, though Manaea’s own small-sample track record caps confidence at a lean.

Ryan Weathers holds a clean SIERA and K-BB% advantage over Sean Burke, who is walking batters at an untenable rate in recent turns. The White Sox counter with a fully rested back end, which narrows the late-inning gap and keeps the edge below the betting threshold.

picks.data_gap: Roof status and confirmed first-pitch weather would clarify PPD probability.

picks.reason: Noah Cameron and Matthew Liberatore are statistical twins by SIERA/xFIP, and the Cardinals’ sizable platoon edge against lefties is the only clear separator. Kansas City’s home field and a rested bullpen balance the ledger, leaving no meaningful edge.

Los Angeles has not announced a starter, which triggers an automatic pass under the data-integrity rules. Gage Jump’s small-sample aggregate for Oakland adds further uncertainty to an already unpriceable game.
picks.data_gap: Confirmed LAA starter and bullpen usage clarity would be required.
